system.docHtml: Piloting is the skill of navigating watercraft through coastal waters, rivers, lakes, and harbors—the practical art of guiding a vessel safely through confined or treacherous passages. Unlike Seamanship, which governs the operation of a ship on open water, Piloting focuses on the intimate knowledge of local waters: channels, shallows, currents, tidal patterns, and landmarks. Skilled pilots are essential for safe passage through unfamiliar harbors and dangerous coastal approaches.
